macro_events.event_summary: Trump to be briefed on potential US strikes against Iran ahead of a 60-day war powers deadline, raising risk of direct Middle East military escalation and Strait of Hormuz disruption.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium on crude oil from potential Strait of Hormuz disruption
Defense procurement / munitions demand from active strike posture
Flight-to-safety into gold and defensive assets

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil with significant upstream leverage; spot Brent rallies on Hormuz risk feed directly into realized prices and Q2 earnings.
High-beta US E&P with Permian crude exposure and elevated leverage — moves more than majors per dollar move in WTI on geopolitical spikes.
Prime contractor for THAAD, PAC-3, and JASSM cruise missiles — exactly the systems used/replenished in any Iran strike package.
Makes Tomahawk cruise missiles (Raytheon) and Patriot interceptors — the primary standoff weapons in any Iran strike scenario, with replenishment orders likely.
B-2 stealth bomber sustainment and B-21 prime; B-2s are the platform for striking hardened Iranian nuclear sites with GBU-57 MOPs.
Drone and tactical missile/target systems supplier; smaller-cap defense names see outsized rallies on conflict headlines vs. primes.

losers.rationale: Highest jet fuel sensitivity among US legacy carriers; transatlantic and Middle East routing also disrupted by airspace closures around Iran/Iraq/Gulf.
Major international exposure including Gulf/India routes that overfly Iranian airspace; both fuel cost and rerouting hit margins.
Domestic carrier but historically unhedged on jet fuel — direct margin compression from any sustained crude spike.
Cruise operators get hit on both bunker fuel costs and Middle East/Mediterranean itinerary cancellations during regional conflict.
Global air freight network with high jet fuel pass-through lag; near-term margin pressure if Brent sustains a war premium.
High-beta growth name vulnerable in risk-off VIX spike; also consumer demand sensitivity if gasoline prices surge meaningfully into summer.
Discretionary consumer + international parks/travel exposure; risk-off plus higher gasoline taxes summer travel demand.

headlines: Trump to get briefed on potential Iran strikes before war hits key 60-day deadline: Reports

macro_events.event_summary: Pre-Apple-earnings speculation (via Kalshi prediction markets) that management will address China exposure and tariffs on the call but avoid foldable iPhone commentary.
primary_drivers: Apple earnings call topic expectations shape supplier and peer positioning
China/tariff commentary risk for Apple supply chain
Absence of foldable iPhone confirmation removes near-term catalyst for foldable-display supply chain
headlines: Apple execs to talk China and tariffs, but will avoid discussing foldable iPhone, per traders on Kalshi

macro_events.event_summary: Analysts warn global oil supply could hit a breaking point if the Iran war extends into June, threatening Strait of Hormuz flows and Iranian crude exports.
primary_drivers: Crude oil price spike from supply disruption risk
Strait of Hormuz shipping/insurance risk premium
Substitution demand toward non-Middle East producers and domestic US shale

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated supermajor with large US/Guyana/Permian upstream production; every $10/bbl move in Brent adds materially to upstream EPS, and XOM has minimal direct Iran exposure.
Similar to XOM — Permian and Kazakhstan-weighted upstream; direct beneficiary of higher realized crude prices with limited Persian Gulf operational risk relative to peers.
Pure-play Permian shale producer with high operating leverage to WTI; supply-shock-driven price spikes flow nearly 1:1 to free cash flow given hedged-light book.
Permian-heavy E&P with high beta to crude; historically among the strongest performers during Middle East supply scares.
High-beta E&P with Egypt and US onshore production; sentiment-driven crude rallies typically push APA up disproportionately given small-cap leverage.
FLEX LNG owns LNG carriers; Hormuz disruption raises charter rates and reroutes gas trade as Europe/Asia scramble for non-Gulf LNG, lifting shipping day rates.
Cheniere is the largest US LNG exporter; Qatari LNG flows through Hormuz are at risk, making US Gulf Coast LNG the marginal substitute and tightening contract spreads.
Oilfield services demand rises with sustained crude above $90; US shale completions activity and pricing power improve as E&Ps redirect cash flow to drilling.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs; a sustained crude spike compresses margins and Delta has limited near-term hedges to offset a fast move.
Same fuel-cost mechanism as DAL; United runs unhedged on jet fuel and has the highest international long-haul exposure, doubly hit by both fuel and Middle East routing/overflight disruption.
Southwest's hedge book is rolled off relative to prior cycles; fuel pass-through to leisure-heavy fares is weak, squeezing margins immediately.
Cruise operator with bunker fuel cost exposure plus discretionary travel demand sensitivity; geopolitical escalation historically suppresses booking curves.
Air freight is fuel-cost intensive and FedEx has limited ability to repass fuel surcharges quickly into contract rates; jet fuel spike hits Express margins.
Naphtha/ethane feedstock costs rise with crude; Dow has limited pricing power in commodity chemicals during a demand-uncertain environment, compressing spreads.
Higher gasoline prices act as a tax on lower-income consumer wallet share, historically compressing discretionary basket size at Walmart even as traffic holds.
Sustained $4+ gasoline historically shifts mix away from high-margin trucks/SUVs which drive GM's North American profitability.

headlines: Oil supplies may hit a breaking point if the Iran war lasts into June

macro_events.event_summary: Major global central banks signal that interest rate hikes are getting closer, indicating a hawkish policy shift.
primary_drivers: Higher discount rate compresses long-duration equity valuations
Wider net interest margins for banks on asset repricing
Stronger USD / higher yields pressure rate-sensitive sectors (REITs, utilities, homebuilders)

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US money-center bank with ~$3.4T assets; rising short-end rates expand NIM via floating-rate commercial loan book repricing faster than deposit costs.
Most asset-sensitive of the big banks per its own 10-Q disclosures — a 100bp parallel shift adds several billion to NII; benefits directly from hawkish repricing.
Earns float on client cash sweeps; higher short rates directly lift net interest revenue, the single largest driver of Schwab earnings.
P&C insurers earn investment income on float; higher reinvestment yields on bond portfolios accrete to earnings as legacy paper rolls off.
Rate-hike cycle drives interest-rate futures/options volume — CME's largest revenue segment is rates complex (SOFR, Treasury futures); hawkish surprises spike trading activity.

losers.rationale: Largest US homebuilder; rising mortgage rates directly suppress affordability and order volumes — homebuilder stocks have tight inverse correlation to 10Y yields.
Realty Income — bond-proxy net-lease REIT with long-dated lease cash flows; trades inversely to real rates and faces higher refinancing costs on its debt stack.
NextEra is a capital-intensive regulated utility + renewables developer with heavy debt funding needs; higher rates raise WACC on long-lived projects and reduce dividend-yield appeal vs Treasuries.

headlines: Big central banks say rate hikes are getting closer
